trim_reasons: CHA is a 16.5-point favorite, creating genuine Q4 rest risk for Ball even at the conservative T10 threshold. With a min_floor of only 21.8 (avg 27.5), a single blowout-shortened game keeps him dangerously close to the line.

The T4 AST floor is strong at 95% overall, but the blowout spread of -16.5 means CHA may run a diluted late-game rotation, compressing Ball's minutes below the floor needed to accumulate 4 assists. Min_floor of 21.8 is thin for a counting stat that requires playmaking opportunities.

Ball is tagged VOLATILE on 3PM, and in a blowout the offense may not generate enough half-court possessions for him to reach 2 made threes before being rested. Iron_floor provides some protection but VOLATILE + blowout compression is the exact combination that produces miss outliers.

As a secondary scorer with no iron_floor, Knueppel's post_miss bounce-back rate of only 72% is the weakest on the slate — combined with a -16.5 blowout spread and a min_floor of 23.7, a garbage-time minutes collapse puts 15 PTS at genuine risk. The bear case here is meaningfully stronger than for Miller.

B2B second night with a b2b_hit_rate_T1 of only 73% — the thinnest B2B rate on the slate — means roughly 1-in-4 B2B games Bane misses even this low floor. On a neutral-momentum team on the road against a mid defense, there is no structural tailwind to offset the B2B suppression.

T6 AST against a tough ORL defense carries the historical lesson directly: tough-defense road AST picks are structurally fragile, and while Harden is at home, ORL's defense suppresses creation. The actual season hit rate at T6 is flagged at 65.1%, meaning the 88% recent-window rate may not be representative of the true underlying distribution.

VOLATILE tag at T15 PTS with a blowout spread (-10.5, favored) and a min_floor of only 26.6 (avg 30.8) — this is the exact volatile-secondary-scorer-in-blowout-favored configuration the audit history flags as a structural disqualifier. The 70% confidence (lowest on slate) combined with VOLATILE and blowout suppression means the floor is contingent on CLE needing Mobley's offense deep into the game, which a 10.5-point spread makes far from guaranteed.
